docString:
If the target of the main goal is a proposition `p`,
`by_contra!` reduces the goal to proving `False` using the additional hypothesis `this : ¬¨ p`.
`by_contra! h` can be used to name the hypothesis `h : ¬¨ p`.
The hypothesis `¬¨ p` will be normalized using `push Not`.
For instance, `¬¨ a < b` will be changed to `b ‚Č§ a`.
`by_contra!` can be used with `rcases` patterns.
For instance, `by_contra! rfl` on `‚äĘ s.Nonempty` will substitute the equality `s = ‚ąÖ`,
and `by_contra! ‚ü®hp, hq‚ü©` on `‚äĘ ¬¨ p ‚ą® ¬¨ q` will introduce `hp : p` and `hq : q`.
`by_contra! h : q` will normalize negations in `¬¨ p`, normalize negations in `q`,
and then check that the two normalized forms are equal.
The resulting hypothesis is the pre-normalized form, `q`.
If the name `h` is not explicitly provided, then `this` will be used as name.
This tactic uses classical reasoning.
It is a variant on the tactic `by_contra`.
Examples:
```lean
example : 1 < 2 := by
  by_contra! h
  -- h : 2 ‚Č§ 1 ‚äĘ False

example : 1 < 2 := by
  by_contra! h : ¬¨ 1 < 2
  -- h : ¬¨ 1 < 2 ‚äĘ False
```

-/
@[tactic_parser 1000]
public meta def byContra! : Lean.ParserDescr‚úĚ :=
  ParserDescr.node‚úĚ `Mathlib.Tactic.ByContra.byContra! 1022
    (ParserDescr.binary‚úĚ `andthen
      (ParserDescr.binary‚úĚ `andthen
        (ParserDescr.binary‚úĚ `andthen (ParserDescr.nonReservedSymbol‚úĚ "by_contra!" false‚úĚ) Lean.Parser.Tactic.optConfig)
        (ParserDescr.unary‚úĚ `optional
          (ParserDescr.binary‚úĚ `andthen
            (ParserDescr.binary‚úĚ `andthen (ParserDescr.const‚úĚ `ppSpace) (ParserDescr.const‚úĚ `colGt))
            Lean.Parser.Tactic.rcasesPatMed)))
      (ParserDescr.unary‚úĚ `optional
        (ParserDescr.binary‚úĚ `andthen (ParserDescr.symbol‚úĚ " : ") (ParserDescr.cat‚úĚ `term 0))))
